title: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman: AI can provide complex medical support, diagnoses Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman talks to Yahoo Finance about a new diagnostic breakthrough, the future of medical AI, and the tech giant's evolving relationship with OpenAI.

Robinhood launches tokens allowing EU users to trade in US stocks The company's shares surged 5.5% and hit a record high after the trading platform said the commission-free tokens can be traded around-the-clock, five days a week.  With the move, Robinhood stands to benefit from rising global interest in the U.S. stock market - home to some of the world's most influential tech giants and leading beneficiaries of the AI boom.  In a January opinion piece for The Washington Post, Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev wrote tokenization could also open the door for retail investors to access private companies' stocks.

Siemens recruits artificial intelligence expert from Amazon ZURICH (Reuters) -Siemens has recruited Amazon executive Vasi Philomin to its new position of head of data and artificial intelligence, the German technology company said on Monday.  The move is the latest step by Siemens as it seeks to develop AI products and applications like its Industrial Copilot.  Siemens has been aiming to accelerate its transition to a technology-focussed company, with AI seen as a key area along with industrial software.

Bill Gates Could've Been The World's First Trillionaire — and Richer Than Elon Musk — If He'd Kept His Microsoft Stock Bill Gates, co-founder of Microsoft, could have been the world’s first trillionaire if he had retained his original company stock. What Happened: The Forbes projection comes in light of Gates’ public commitment to donate nearly all of his remaining wealth and wind down the Gates Foundation by 2045. Gates, along with his ex-wife Melinda, have already donated $60.2 billion to the foundation since its inception in 2000, making them the second-largest philanthropists in America. Will This "Magnificent Seven" Stock Overtake Microsoft and Nvidia As the Largest Company in the World By 2030? Amazon has two key growth segments.  Its investment thesis centers on profit growth, rather than revenue growth.  While Nvidia and Microsoft are duking it out to see which company will maintain its position as the largest in the world by market cap, another competitor is lurking in the background: Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN).
